capability_objective: Resolve Reykjavík research trip in Reykjavík and Chicago volunteer summit in Chicago with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Chicago volunteer summit is still draft, cancel only Chicago volunteer summit, with reason "Traveler chose a different itinerary". Leave Reykjavík research trip unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify travel coordinator with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Report only returned fields, and label any missing requested detail as unknown. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two travel records in the supplied sandbox through the genuine local `./travelctl` executable, using Pi's Bash tool. Read `TRAVEL.md`, then inspect `./travelctl --help` to learn the executable's interface. Treat the executable as the only interface to travel data. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its runtime database, operation journal, receipt, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence or create a helper script, function, loop, or wrapper for the workflow.

Search for the exact trip name `Reykjavík research trip` in `Reykjavík` and the exact trip name `Chicago volunteer summit` in `Chicago`. Start the independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one travel operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or begin retrieval until both calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, cancellation, and notification; identify the unresolved lookup and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start the retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes `travelctl`, again with exactly one travel operation in each call. Use only the IDs returned by the corresponding searches and wait for both retrievals. If either full record is missing, does not match its search result, or lacks a status, stop before cancellation and notification. Base the condition only on the complete retrieved Chicago record, never on a search summary.

If and only if the retrieved Chicago record's status is exactly `draft`, cancel only its returned stable ID with reason `Traveler chose a different itinerary`. Otherwise do not cancel anything. Leave Reykjavík research trip and every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-location record unchanged. Only if the cancellation command returns a successful mutation, send exactly one later notification to `travel coordinator`, using the exact nonempty outcome returned by that cancellation. If cancellation is skipped, fails, or does not report a successful mutation, do not notify. Do not list records, read profiles, check availability, create, update, or perform any other cancellation or notification. Do not write travel state or audit evidence directly.

After the permitted path completes, create `travel-outcome.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly six newline-terminated, nonblank lines with no heading or extra prose. Use this exact structure:
`| Name | ID | Location | Date | Retrieved status |`
`| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |`
`| <Reykjavík record's returned name> | <Reykjavík record's returned ID> | <Reykjavík record's returned location> | <Reykjavík record's returned date> | <Reykjavík record's returned status> |`
`| <Chicago record's returned name> | <Chicago record's returned ID> | <Chicago record's returned location> | <Chicago record's returned date> | <Chicago record's returned status> |`
`Cancellation: <returned ID> | <returned status> | <returned cancellation reason> | <returned outcome>`
`Notification: <returned recipient> | <returned delivered value> | <returned outcome>`
Use only fields returned by the successful full-record, cancellation, and notification commands. Preserve returned strings exactly. Render every absent or null requested field as the lowercase word `unknown`; preserve a returned JSON boolean as lowercase `true` or `false`. Do not include planner, notes, unrelated records, or any other fields. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the file, leave supplied and protected files unchanged, and make the final assistant response exactly the file's content.
